### 970. `POST` `/api/v1/connectors/dast/ingest/zap`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/connectors/dast/ingest/zap

**Tags:** connectors, dast

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Ingest a ZAP JSON report and persist findings to SecurityFindingsEngine.  Use this when you have a
ZAP scan output already (e.g. CI artefact) and want to bring its findings into ALDECI without re-
running ZAP.

**Request Body:** `DastIngestZapRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `org_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `report` | Dict | Yes |  | Parsed ZAP JSON report (zap-baseline.py -J output) |
| `target` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `scan_id` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `mirror_to_bug_bounty` | bool | No | True |  |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

**Description:**

Add a new SIEM forwarding target.

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `name` | str | Yes |  | Human-readable target name |
| `transport` | str | Yes |  | syslog_tcp, syslog_udp, splunk_hec, webhook |
| `output_format` | str | No | cef | cef, leef, json |
| `host` | str | No | localhost | Target host (syslog) |
| `port` | int | No | 514 | Target port (syslog) |
| `url` | str | No |  | URL (Splunk HEC / webhook) |
| `token` | str | No |  | Auth token (Splunk HEC / webhook) |
| `index` | str | No | fixops | Splunk index |
| `source` | str | No | aldeci-ctem | Source identifier |
| `sourcetype` | str | No | aldeci:security | Sourcetype |
| `enabled` | bool | No | True |  |
| `event_filters` | List | No | PydanticUndefined | Event types to forward (empty=all) |

**Responses:**

### 1028. `POST` `/api/v1/auto-pentest/run`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/auto-pentest/run

**Tags:** Auto Pentest

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Run all 8 pentest suites against ALDECI itself.  Uses FastAPI TestClient internally — no real
network calls. Results are stored by run_id for later retrieval.

**Request Body:** `RunRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `base_url` | str | No | http://testserver | Base URL to pentest. Use 'http://testserver' for self-test mode. |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `RunResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `run_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `started_at` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `base_url` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `status` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `total_probes` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `vulnerable_count` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `report` | Dict | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1050. `POST` `/api/v1/breach-sim/run`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/breach-sim/run

**Tags:** breach-simulation

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Run a breach simulation scenario against current defenses.  Executes all attack steps for the chosen
scenario, evaluates which defenses trigger, and returns a scored SimulationResult with gaps and
timing metrics. Result is persisted to SQLite.

**Request Body:** `RunSimulationRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `scenario` | AttackScenario | Yes |  | Attack scenario to simulate |
| `org_id` | str | Yes |  | Organisation identifier |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `SimulationResultResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `scenario` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `steps_executed` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `steps_blocked` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `detection_time_seconds` | float | Yes |  |  |
| `containment_time_seconds` | float | Yes |  |  |
| `data_at_risk` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `defenses_tested` | List | Yes |  |  |
| `gaps_found` | List | Yes |  |  |
| `score` | float | Yes |  |  |
| `org_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `simulated_at` | str | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

| `org_id` | str | Yes |  | Organisation identifier |

| `name` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `template_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `target_emails` | List | Yes |  |  |
| `sent_count` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `opened_count` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `clicked_count` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `reported_count` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `started_at` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `ended_at` | Optional | Yes |  |  |
| `org_id` | str | Yes |  |  |

**Summary:** GET /api/v1/phishing/users/susceptibility

**Tags:** phishing-simulation

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Return an individual employee's phishing susceptibility score (0.0–1.0).  Score is calculated as:
clicks / campaigns_targeted. Risk levels: low (<0.25) / medium (0.25–0.5) / high (0.5–0.75) /
critical (≥0.75).

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `email` | query | str | Yes | Employee email address |
| `org_id` | query | str | Yes | Organisation identifier |

**200 OK** — `Dict`

### 1070. `POST` `/api/v1/hunt/start`

**Summary:** Start a new hunt

**Tags:** Threat Hunting

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Start a structured threat hunt workflow for the specified hypothesis.  Creates a new hunt in ACTIVE
status, recording trigger type and context. Returns the hunt ID for subsequent status queries.

**Request Body:** `StartHuntRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `hypothesis_id` | str | Yes |  | ID of the hypothesis to hunt against |
| `org_id` | str | No | default | Organisation ID |
| `analyst` | str | No | system | Analyst name or user ID |
| `trigger_type` | HuntTriggerType | No | HuntTriggerType.MANUAL | What initiated this hunt |
| `trigger_context` | Dict | No | PydanticUndefined | Additional context about the trigger |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `StartHuntResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `hunt_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `hypothesis_name` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `status` | HuntStatus | Yes |  |  |
| `message` | str | No | Hunt started |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

built-in profiles (APT28, APT29, Lazarus Group, FIN7, Volt Typhoon).

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `motivation` | query | Optional | No | Filter by motivation (financial, espionage, hacktivism, sabotage) |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `ActorsResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `actors` | List | Yes |  |  |
| `total` | int | Yes |  |  |

### 1078. `GET` `/api/v1/hunt/kill-chain`

**Summary:** Kill chain coverage visualization

**Tags:** Threat Hunting

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Show coverage across all 7 Cyber Kill Chain phases.  For each phase, returns: hypothesis count,
sigma rule count, active hunt count, and whether coverage exists. Highlights gaps where no detection
hypotheses are defined.

**200 OK** — `KillChainResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |

### 1081. `POST` `/api/v1/hunt/trigger`

**Summary:** Fire an automated hunt trigger

**Tags:** Threat Hunting

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Fire an automated trigger to initiate a hunt.  Trigger types: new_cve, ioc_match, network_anomaly,
compliance_failure. Automatically selects the best matching hypothesis and starts a hunt. Returns
the started hunt details, or null if no matching hypothesis found.

**Request Body:** `FireTriggerRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `trigger_type` | HuntTriggerType | Yes |  |  |
| `context` | Dict | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `org_id` | str | No | default |  |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Optional`

| `title` | str | Yes |  | Short vulnerability title |
| `description` | str | Yes |  | Detailed vulnerability description |
| `poc_steps` | str | No |  | Step-by-step proof-of-concept reproduction |
| `impact_assessment` | str | No |  | Reporter's assessment of business impact |
| `attachments` | List | No | PydanticUndefined | Attachment filenames or URLs |
| `org_id` | str | No | default | Organisation ID |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `VulnerabilitySubmission`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|

| `reporter_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `reporter_email` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `reporter_name` | str | No |  |  |

| `title` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `description` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `poc_steps` | str | No |  | Proof-of-concept reproduction steps |
| `impact_assessment` | str | No |  | Reporter's impact assessment |
| `attachments` | List | No | PydanticUndefined | Attachment URLs or filenames |
| `status` | SubmissionStatus | No | SubmissionStatus.NEW |  |
| `severity` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `cvss_score` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `duplicate_of` | Optional | No | None | ID of original submission if duplicate |
| `aldeci_finding_id` | Optional | No | None | Linked ALDECI finding ID |
| `reward_id` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `submitted_at` | str | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `acknowledged_at` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `triaged_at` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `resolved_at` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `sla_deadline` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `triage_notes` | str | No |  |  |
| `org_id` | str | No | default |  |

### 1105. `POST` `/api/v1/deception/check`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/deception/check

**Tags:** deception

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Check whether a value matches any active canary token.  Called by other engines (connectors,
scanners) to detect credential theft. Returns matched=True and alert details if a canary was
triggered.

**Request Body:** `CheckCanaryRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `token_value` | str | Yes |  | Value to check against known canaries |
| `source_ip` | str | Yes |  | IP address of the accessor |
| `context` | Dict | No | PydanticUndefined | Optional context (user_agent, headers, etc.) |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `CheckCanaryResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `matched` | bool | Yes |  |  |
| `alert` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `message` | str | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1181. `POST` `/api/v1/connectors/ti/sync`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/connectors/ti/sync

**Tags:** connectors-threat-intel

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Run all enabled adapters then cross-correlate IoCs against tenant findings.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `org_id` | query | str | No | default |

**Request Body:** `SyncRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `run_misp` | bool | No | True | Pull from MISP feeds |
| `run_circl` | bool | No | True | Pull from CIRCL CVE feed |
| `run_phishtank` | bool | No | True | Pull from PhishTank |
| `run_otx` | bool | No | True | Pull from AlienVault OTX |
| `run_ghsa` | bool | No | True | Pull from GitHub Advisory Database (GHSA) |
| `run_correlation` | bool | No | True | Cross-correlate IoCs against tenant findings |
| `misp_feed_urls` | Optional | No | None | Override default MISP feed URLs (max 10). |

**Responses:**


**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1237. `POST` `/api/v1/ndr/flows`

**Summary:** Ingest a network flow

**Tags:** ndr

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Ingest a network flow, score its risk, and auto-alert if risk > 0.7.

**Request Body:** `IngestFlowRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `src_ip` | str | No |  | Source IP address |
| `dst_ip` | str | No |  | Destination IP address |
| `src_port` | int | No | 0 |  |
| `dst_port` | int | No | 0 |  |
| `protocol` | str | No | TCP | Protocol (TCP/UDP/ICMP/DNS/HTTP/HTTPS/SSH/RDP) |
| `bytes_sent` | int | No | 0 |  |
| `bytes_recv` | int | No | 0 |  |
| `duration_ms` | int | No | 0 |  |
| `flow_type` | str | No | internal | internal/external/lateral/exfiltration_suspect/c2_suspect |
| `mitre_technique` | str | No |  |  |
| `observed_at` | Optional | No | None |  |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1276. `POST` `/api/v1/connectors/falcon/ingest`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/connectors/falcon/ingest

**Tags:** CrowdStrike Falcon Connector

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Ingest a Falcon Detection.Created JSON dump.  Provide either ``events`` (parsed list) or
``json_text`` (raw text). Returns counts (ingested/failed/findings_recorded/edr_events/
correlation_events) and the list of detection_ids absorbed.

**Request Body:** `FalconIngestRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `org_id` | str | No | default |  |
| `events` | Optional | No | None | List of Falcon Detection.Created event dicts. |
| `json_text` | Optional | No | None | Raw JSON string (array, single object, or NDJSON). |
| `max_events` | Optional | No | None | Optional cap on number of events to process. |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1396. `POST` `/api/v1/siem/ingest`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/siem/ingest

**Tags:** siem-integration

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Parse and ingest a raw syslog or CEF log line as a SIEM event.  Accepts: - Syslog RFC 3164:
``<PRI>Mmm DD HH:MM:SS hostname tag: message`` - Syslog RFC 5424: ``<PRI>VERSION TIMESTAMP HOSTNAME
APP-NAME PROCID MSGID ...`` - CEF: ``CEF:0|Vendor|Product|Version|SigID|Name|Severity|extensions`` -
format="auto" (default) detects CEF by presence of "CEF:" prefix.  Returns the normalised, stored
event record including parsed fields.

**Request Body:** `RawIngestIn`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `org_id` | str | No | default |  |
| `raw` | str | Yes |  | Raw syslog (RFC 3164/5424) or CEF log line |
| `format` | str | No | auto | 'syslog' \| 'cef' \| 'auto' (default — auto-detected from content) |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1413. `GET` `/api/v1/siem-output/history/{target_id}`

**Summary:** GET /api/v1/siem-output/history/{target_id}

**Tags:** siem-output

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Get delivery history for a specific SIEM target.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `target_id` | path | str | Yes | — |
| `org_id` | query | str | No | default |
| `limit` | query | int | No | 50 |

**Responses:**


**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/connectors/siem/detect

**Tags:** siem-connector

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Auto-detect the SIEM format of a payload.

**Request Body:** `DetectRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `payload` | Any | Yes |  | Raw payload to detect format of |

**Responses:**

---

### 1416. `POST` `/api/v1/connectors/siem/ingest`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/connectors/siem/ingest

**Tags:** siem-connector

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Parse a SIEM payload and mirror to SIEM, correlation, and findings engines.  Accepts: - Splunk HEC
envelope (single or NDJSON batch) - Datadog Logs API JSON - Microsoft Sentinel KQL JSON result -
Elasticsearch _bulk NDJSON - Wazuh alerts.json record(s) - Suricata eve.json record(s) - CEF lines
(used by QRadar, ArcSight) - RFC 3164/5424 syslog lines - Generic JSON-Lines  With format=auto
(default), the adapter is auto-detected.

**Request Body:** `IngestRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `org_id` | str | No | default | Tenant identifier |
| `payload` | Any | Yes |  | Raw SIEM payload (str, dict, or list) |
| `format` | str | No | auto | Adapter key — one of: splunk_hec \| datadog \| sentinel_kql \| elk_bulk \| wazuh_alert \| suricata_eve \| cef \| syslog \| json_lines \| auto |
| `source_id` | Optional | No | None | Optional SIEM source ID |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1417. `POST` `/api/v1/connectors/siem/generate`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/connectors/siem/generate

**Tags:** siem-connector

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Generate realistic SIEM fixture events without ingesting them.  Returns ``tenants *
events_per_tenant`` synthetic SIEM payloads spanning Splunk HEC, Datadog, Sentinel KQL, ELK, Wazuh,
CEF, syslog, Suricata. Useful for connector smoke testing without DB writes.

**Request Body:** `GenerateRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `tenants` | int | No | 15 | Number of tenants to generate for |
| `events_per_tenant` | int | No | 14 | Events per tenant (10-20 typical) |
| `seed` | int | No | 1337 | RNG seed for deterministic output |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1418. `POST` `/api/v1/connectors/siem/tail`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/connectors/siem/tail

**Tags:** siem-connector

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Tail real log files on disk and ingest new bytes since last call.  Designed for /var/log/system.log,
ALDECI's own structlog JSON output, or any other newline-delimited log file. Per-file byte cursors
persist in ``.aldeci/siem_tail_cursors.json`` so subsequent calls only ingest *new* content (real
incremental log tailing, not re-ingest).  Each file's content is auto-detected (JSON-leading →
json_lines, else syslog), parsed by the matching adapter, and mirrored into the SIEM, correlation,
and findings engines.

**Request Body:** `TailRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `org_id` | str | No | default | Tenant identifier |
| `file_paths` | List | Yes |  | Absolute paths to log files to tail (e.g. /var/log/system.log). |
| `format` | str | No | auto | Adapter key per file (auto picks json_lines for JSON-leading lines, syslog otherwise). |
| `max_bytes_per_file` | int | No | 1048576 |  |
| `max_lines_per_file` | int | No | 5000 |  |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1419. `POST` `/api/v1/connectors/siem/generate-and-ingest`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/connectors/siem/generate-and-ingest

**Tags:** siem-connector

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Generate fixture events and ingest them into all three engines.  Used by E2E pipeline tests to seed
realistic, multi-tenant, multi-format SIEM data. Returns per-format and per-tenant counts plus
aggregate totals.

**Request Body:** `GenerateRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `tenants` | int | No | 15 | Number of tenants to generate for |
| `events_per_tenant` | int | No | 14 | Events per tenant (10-20 typical) |
| `seed` | int | No | 1337 | RNG seed for deterministic output |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1512. `POST` `/api/v1/threat-exposure/correlations`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/threat-exposure/correlations

**Tags:** Threat Exposure

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Correlate a threat with an asset.

**Request Body:** `CorrelateThreatRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `org_id` | str | No | default |  |
| `asset_id` | str | Yes |  | Asset to correlate threat against |
| `threat_id` | Optional | No | None | Threat identifier (auto-generated if omitted) |
| `threat_type` | str | No | exploit | malware/apt/ransomware/phishing/exploit/insider |
| `confidence` | float | No | 50.0 | Confidence 0-100 |
| `severity` | str | No | medium | critical/high/medium/low |
| `ioc_matched` | bool | No | False | Whether an IOC matched |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

| `first_seen` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `last_seen` | Optional | No | None |  |

### 1602. `POST` `/api/v1/threat-attribution/actors`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/threat-attribution/actors

**Tags:** Threat Attribution

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Create a new threat actor profile.

**Request Body:** `CreateActorRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `org_id` | str | No | default | Organisation identifier |
| `name` | str | Yes |  | Threat actor name (required) |
| `actor_type` | str | No | unknown | Type: nation_state, criminal_group, hacktivist, insider, competitor, unknown |
| `aliases` | List | No | PydanticUndefined | Known aliases / alternate names |
| `origin_country` | str | No |  | Country of origin (ISO-3166 code) |
| `motivation` | str | No |  | Primary motivation (e.g. espionage, financial) |
| `sophistication` | str | No | basic | Sophistication level: advanced, moderate, basic |
| `active` | bool | No | True | Whether the actor is currently active |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1622. `POST` `/api/v1/alert-triage/bulk-triage`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/alert-triage/bulk-triage

**Tags:** Alert Triage

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Apply the same triage action to multiple alerts at once.  Accepts either ``alert_ids`` (list) or
``alert_id`` (single string). ``org_id`` may be provided as a query parameter or in the request
body. Valid actions: acknowledge, ack, resolve, false_positive, escalate.  Validation contract:   *
422 — request shape invalid (missing IDs, empty list, bad enum, missing org).   * 403 — at least one
ID belongs to a different org (cross-tenant attempt).   * 404 — at least one ID does not exist
anywhere.   * 200 — all IDs validated and updated atomically.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `org_id` | query | Optional | No | Organization ID |

**Request Body:** `BulkTriageRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `alert_ids` | Optional | No | None | List of alert IDs to action (1-500 entries) |
| `alert_id` | Optional | No | None | Single alert ID (convenience alias for alert_ids) |
| `action` | Literal | Yes |  | acknowledge \| ack \| resolve \| false_positive \| escalate |
| `org_id` | Optional | No | None | Organization ID (can also be passed as query param) |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1626. `POST` `/api/v1/alert-triage/investigate/{alert_id}`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/alert-triage/investigate/{alert_id}

**Tags:** Alert Triage

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

SOC analyst investigation endpoint.  Correlates an alert across all security domains and returns: -
The alert record with full metadata - Related alerts from the same source/severity in last 24 h -
Affected assets extracted from raw_alert_json (host, ip, user) - Incident history matching those
assets - IOC summary (IPs, domains, hashes) parsed from raw payload - TrustGraph GraphRAG cross-
domain context - Recommended IR playbook based on source/severity heuristics

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `alert_id` | path | str | Yes | — |
| `org_id` | query | str | Yes | Organization ID |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

| `severity` | str | No | medium | critical \| high \| medium \| low |
| `description` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `frequency_score` | Optional | No | 50 |  |
| `impact_score` | Optional | No | 50 |  |
| `first_observed` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `last_observed` | Optional | No | None |  |

### 1636. `POST` `/api/v1/threat-vectors/import-mitre`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/threat-vectors/import-mitre

**Tags:** Threat Vector Analysis

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Import MITRE ATT&CK techniques from STIX 2.1 bundle into the local DB.  Downloads the enterprise-
attack bundle from MITRE CTI GitHub (~10 MB), extracts all attack-pattern objects (techniques + sub-
techniques), and upserts them into data/mitre_attack.db.  Returns
technique/subtechnique/tactic/platform counts.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `org_id` | query | str | Yes | Organization ID |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

**Summary:** GET /api/v1/threat-vectors/mitre-techniques

**Tags:** Threat Vector Analysis

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

List imported MITRE ATT&CK techniques from the local DB.  Optionally filter by tactic slug (e.g.
execution, initial-access). Returns 404 with a hint if no techniques have been imported yet.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `tactic` | query | Optional | No | Filter by tactic slug, e.g. initial-access |

**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1721. `POST` `/api/v1/actor-tracking/actors/import-mitre`

**Summary:** Import threat actors from public MITRE ATT&CK enterprise dataset

**Tags:** Threat Actor Tracking

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Pull intrusion-set objects from MITRE ATT&CK STIX bundle and register each as a tracked actor for
this org. Idempotent: dedupes on actor_name. Returns import summary + sample records.  Source:
https://github.com/mitre/cti (Apache-2.0 licensed public data).

**Request Body:** `ImportMitreModel`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `org_id` | str | No | default |  |
| `limit` | Optional | No | None | Cap number of actors imported (None = all ~150 MITRE groups) |
| `cached_path` | Optional | No | None | Optional local path to cached enterprise-attack.json (skips network fetch) |

**Responses:**


**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|

| `hunt_type` | str | Yes |  | hypothesis \| ioc \| anomaly \| behavioral \| threat-actor \| ttp \| situational |
| `threat_category` | str | Yes |  | Threat category being hunted |
| `mitre_technique` | str | No |  | MITRE ATT&CK technique ID |
| `hypothesis` | str | No |  | Primary hunt hypothesis |
| `data_sources` | Optional | No | None | Data sources required |
| `tools` | Optional | No | None | Tools used in this hunt |

**Auth:** API Key required

https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/archive/refs/heads/master.tar.gz, parses all YAML rules under
rules/ (skipping deprecated/unsupported/tests), and upserts them by UUID.  Returns a summary with
rule count by level and platform.

### 1797. `POST` `/api/v1/sigmahq/import`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/sigmahq/import

**Tags:** SigmaHQ

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Download and import all SigmaHQ detection rules from master branch.  Downloads
https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/archive/refs/heads/master.tar.gz, extracts the rules/ directory,
and upserts all YAML rules into the local sigmahq_rules.db.  Skips tests/, deprecated/, and
unsupported/ sub-dirs.  Returns a summary with total rule count broken down by severity level and
platform.

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

**Tags:** SigmaHQ

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

List SigmaHQ detection rules from the local DB with optional filters.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `level` | query | Optional | No | Filter by severity level: informational \| low \| medium \| high \| critical. 'high' also returns critical rules. |
| `technique` | query | Optional | No | Filter by ATT&CK technique substring, e.g. t1059.001 |
| `platform` | query | Optional | No | Filter by logsource product/platform, e.g. windows \| linux \| web |
| `limit` | query | int | No | 500 |
| `offset` | query | int | No | 0 |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)

### 1800. `POST` `/api/v1/dbir/import`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/dbir/import

**Tags:** DBIR

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Download and import the VERIS Community Database master archive.  Pulls https://github.com/vz-
risk/VCDB/archive/refs/heads/master.tar.gz, walks data/json/validated/, parses each incident JSON,
and upserts into the local dbir.db keyed by incident_id.  Returns a summary keyed by action_pattern,
actor type, and victim NAICS.

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

| `industry_naics` | query | Optional | No | Filter by victim industry NAICS prefix (e.g. '52' for Finance) |

| `dread_score` | Optional | No | None | DREAD risk score |
| `affected_component` | str | Yes |  | System component at risk |
| `mitigations` | List | No | PydanticUndefined | Mitigation controls applied |
| `status` | ThreatStatus | No | ThreatStatus.IDENTIFIED | Current threat status |
| `org_id` | str | No | default | Organisation identifier |
| `created_at` | datetime | No | PydanticUndefined |  |

### 1851. `POST` `/api/v1/threat-models/threats/{threat_id}/score`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/threat-models/threats/{threat_id}/score

**Tags:** threat-modeling

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Calculate and store DREAD score for a threat.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `threat_id` | path | str | Yes | — |

**Request Body:** `ScoreThreatRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `damage` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `reproducibility` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `exploitability` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `affected_users` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `discoverability` | int | Yes |  |  |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `DREADScore`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `damage` | int | Yes |  | Damage potential if exploited (1-10) |
| `reproducibility` | int | Yes |  | How easily can the attack be reproduced (1-10) |
| `exploitability` | int | Yes |  | Skill/effort required to exploit (1-10) |
| `affected_users` | int | Yes |  | Number of users affected (1-10) |
| `discoverability` | int | Yes |  | How easy is it to discover the vulnerability (1-10) |
| `total` | float | No | 0.0 | Computed mean of all five dimensions |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

| `id` | str | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `name` | str | Yes |  | Threat model name |
| `system_description` | str | Yes |  | Description of the system being modeled |
| `data_flow_description` | str | No |  | Data flow summary (DFD narrative) |
| `trust_boundaries` | List | No | PydanticUndefined | Trust boundary labels |
| `threats` | List | No | PydanticUndefined | List of ThreatEntry IDs |
| `created_at` | datetime | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `org_id` | str | No | default | Organisation identifier |

### 1856. `POST` `/api/v1/threat-models/{model_id}/threats`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/threat-models/{model_id}/threats

**Tags:** threat-modeling

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Add a threat entry to an existing threat model.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `model_id` | path | str | Yes | — |

**Request Body:** `AddThreatRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `title` | str | Yes |  | Short threat title |
| `description` | str | Yes |  | Detailed threat description |
| `stride_category` | STRIDECategory | Yes |  | STRIDE category |
| `affected_component` | str | Yes |  | Component at risk |
| `org_id` | str | No | default | Organisation identifier |
| `status` | ThreatStatus | No | ThreatStatus.IDENTIFIED | Initial status |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `AddThreatResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `threat_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `message` | str | No | Threat added |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1858. `POST` `/api/v1/tor-exit/import`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/tor-exit/import

**Tags:** Tor Exit Nodes

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Pull the TorProject bulk exit list and replace the local store.  The list is fetched from
https://check.torproject.org/torbulkexitlist. Replace semantics: each call is idempotent — the list
IS the new state.  Returns:     {"ips": N, "imported_at": "<iso8601>"}

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

| `limit` | query | int | No | Max IPs to return |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`
